title: The Evolution of Context Engineering: From Prompt Hacking to Cognitive Architectures

sections.title: The Context Memory Problem
key_points: AI assistants fail to maintain context across conversations despite having sufficient memory capacity
Example: AI forgets user's wife's preferences (sustainable fashion, rock climbing) after 3 weeks
Models have 128,000 token memory but only need 2,000 tokens for the conversation
The issue is not model capability but poor context engineering architecture

sections.title: Evolution from Prompt Hacking to Cognitive Architectures
key_points: Article title suggests progression from basic prompt manipulation to sophisticated cognitive systems
Context engineering has become more valuable than the underlying model itself
Need for proper memory system architecture in AI applications
Gap between model capabilities and practical implementation

summary.title: Context Engineering: The Critical Gap Between AI Model Capability and Practical Memory Management
takeaways: Modern AI models have sufficient memory capacity but lack proper context engineering architecture
Context engineering is becoming more valuable than the underlying model capabilities
Poor memory system design leads to frustrating user experiences despite advanced AI technology
The field is evolving from simple prompt manipulation to sophisticated cognitive architectures
actionable: Design proper memory systems that persist important context across conversations
Architect context management systems rather than relying solely on model capabilities
Implement structured approaches to context retention and retrieval
Focus on context engineering as a key differentiator in AI applications
